review_title: Aiven for PostgreSQL by Darko Britvec
description: I like Aiven and I think I would keep using it for both business and personal projects.
author: Darko B.
pros: I like how easy it is to create and manage Postgres instance using Aiven + it's not that expensive in comparison to other providers.
cons: I would like to have more insights in query performance.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The general ease of operation for: - Spinning up new databases - Scaling up - Scaling down - Migrating to a different region - Restoring backups Is really great. It's amazing how many features you get out of the box. The plans with redundancy are also cool because you get full access to the slave as a read-only replica, this means you can run heavy select queries without putting load on the main database. There is a very significant "it just works" factor to it, which is not something I would imagine myself saying about a database provider so easily.
cons: They are not the cheapest Monitoring/alerting options were a bit lackluste, but this has improved recently.

author: Mikko P.
cons: Not being able to copy from one gb to another easily or automatically

description: A good program that saves a lot of time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Good software for automating database operations, has good features to facilitate connections, completely secures data, and offers a free trial.
cons: Sometimes problems occur in copying operations, this platform needs to be updated to increase work efficiency.

review_title: It's great. Everything just works; setup was effortless; customer service is responsive and helpful.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The best thing are the no-downtime migrations and upgrades. The setup was quick and easy and since then no maintenance has been required. Customer service is great and pro-active. The dashboard works well.
cons: As far as I can tell there's no way to set a separate billing email. It would be nice to also see in the dashboard what's taking up most memory and disk space and to be able to set up alerts, such as if there are queries that take too long to execute.

review_title: Works well, has cloud datacenters in many regions.
description: Don't have to host Linux servers for it myself.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Datacenters in multiple regions, multiple PostgreSQL versions. General high level of availability. Support is helpful and pretty fast (located in my timezone.)
cons: No real cons actually. Had a problem once (which was related to my PG driver etc), the customer support helped me resolve it fairly quickly.

description: I am very satisfied with Aiven so far.It eliminated the need for in-house solutions for backup, scaling and upgrading a PostgreSQL server.
author: Márton S.
pros: I have only used the PostgreSQL service so far and I really like how easy it is to start, fork en upgrade the service. A great feature, and a must for us, is that you can choose on which cloud provider and location the service should be deployed. We have been hosting our own PostgreSQL server for years and had to manage upgrades, backup and scaling on our own. And it feels great to finally leave that behind us and start using PostgreSQL as a service. The support is great to. I have been in contact with the support using the chat feature and have been helped every time.
cons: The official API client was a bit hard to understand at first.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: The best managed PostgreSQL solution I've tried
description: I have always wanted a solution like Aiven; easy-to-use like Heroku, but with far more powerful features and far more affordable.
author: Charlie O.
pros: - latest version of PostgreSQL available nearly immediately - great performance - easy-to-understand UI with enough power to do everything I needed - straightforward solution, not excessively complicated - ability to deploy into cloud provider of my choice, no lock-in - clear pricing - excellent support
cons: - not possible to leverage free credits from cloud providers
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: James W.
pros: Can't fault Aiven at all, great service, the exact services we require (postgres and elasticsearch) and always more than willing to help
cons: While it is a managed service you do need to have at least a minimum of knowledge to setup and run the services on offer.

description: Extremely esay to use and get started with in seconds. Very helpful support if so be needed and a very reliable service.
author: Mathias P.
pros: It's incredibly easy to spin up a postgreSQL instance and start being productive. Everything from creating a secure password to SSL-certificates are handled automatically. Point in time backups make you confident to use and test without gambling hours of work and for the serious tests and development environments - spin up a replica by forking the database with the press of a button!
cons: It is a bit expensive if you need more disk space, which isn't unusual when dealing with data and databases.

description: Mi experiencia es muy satisfactoria, lo recomiendo totalmente
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Lo que más me gusta es la facilidad de uso, poder trabajar de forma segura y rápido
cons: No tengo ningún contra. Recomiendo el programa

author: AURELIEN D.
pros: Easy to use and manage for other users and device declaration
cons: Nothing to report now, the management of authorized ip is not the most user-friendly, but good enough.

review_title: Reliable component of our data warehouse
description: As a data team, we are interested in having all the data, experimenting with various methods of transformations and insight extractions without interfering with the raw data. Aiven provides a suitable and reliable environment with excellent performance.
author: Ali A.
pros: Simplicity and ease of use. Integrates well as a middle component of the data pipeline: source to Aiven to multiple destinations. No issues with performance.
cons: nothing
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Nice UI. Great support. Great features (for PG, a bit less for redis unfortunately)
author: Yoav A.
pros: Setup is very intuitive, and it seems to work great. Support was prompt and knowledgeable (which is hugely important). PG features seem solid and the flexibility to choose the cloud hosting, size etc is great.
cons: easy Redis backup/import is still missing unfortunately. We'd love to use Aiven for redis too, but with those limitations we can't yet. I'm still a bit hesitant about how likely Aiven is to stay in business as a company. It's not so well known and hard to know who's behind it and that it's doing well financially to keep going for many years. (btw, I think you guys should be more active on HackerNews and other geek channels where you should market yourselves.)

author: Alex G.
pros: Easy to deploy and setup replication on any cloud provider we like
cons: We want to move to our production environment but we need a SOC2. Currently, aiven does not have that. WHEN WILL YOU GET IT?!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Jim W.
pros: -Ease of deployment and a centralized place to manage multiple databases and services. -Ability to choose region to deploy in
cons: - I still had credits left but my trial ended at the 30 day mark. This wasn't a huge deal and I saw the credits role over but typically with credit based trials (AWS GCP Azure) you can go until your credits are used up.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Great PostgreSQL offering with managed PGBouncher
author: Andy S.
pros: - Managed PGBouncher - easy setup and upgrade - quicky offering new technologies (e.g. PostgreSQL 10)
cons: - lack of integration with datadog for metrics. right now we need to get some metrics manually using the Aiven metrics API

review_title: We love to use Aiven to spawn PostgreSQL databases, it's easy to use and many features we like
description: Fast and easy setup, vast choice of cloud providers and regions.
author: Louis B.
pros: Zero-downtime migrations and plan changes, database forks, nice metrics with iops. Configuration seems to be hand-crafted to the installation, which is nice.
cons: Plans could be more flexible in terms of space. Would be great to be able to choose disk space independently. Even for a small database which is used to store archive data, it gets very expensive.

author: Justin K.
pros: Terraform compatibility, Easy to deploy, fast and scalable
cons: Potential Slow support due to the chat saying that "turn around time is 3 -5 hours".
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Saurabh B.
pros: Cloud Version.Local SSDs, faster than our previous postgresSQl.
cons: Couldn't find anything so far but will def point out if I find in near future.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Andrey S.
pros: I enjoyed using the Aiven console a lot. It is very straightforward to use, well-organized and documented.
cons: Not much to complain about. Probably Services overview page could provide connection details straight away, so the user doesn't have to click on the service name to find the host and port.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Very good service, easy to use
author: Sylvain L.
pros: Aiven services on PostgresSQL is the easier I ever met. the admin UI is user friendly, and the whole thing is secured.
cons: The time of start and stop is quiet long, but it's really nothing so far.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It has allowed us to effectively step stone our progression to Citus DB by starting off with Timescale. However, we most likely will leave it later once we reach > $1000/month in DB spending just because the pricing is high and Citus seems to provide more flexibility and performance for a lower price point. We strongly dislike the lack of super-user access for the DB, as it messes up our tooling. We found while the support & interface, as well as the performance in GCP cloud, were great - we ran into various edge case/bugs, particularly with the hobbyist plan. I would not recommend the platform for anything but Postgres or Redis.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The ability to use the TimescaleDB Postgres Extension.
cons: The Lack of Super-User/Postgres Account access.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Andrei T.
pros: I really like UI/UX. It is clean enough!
cons: Not enough options. Like, need filter metrics by specific dates (not only by d/w/m/y). Or sort current queries by time.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Aiven is the perfect, no-lock-in DB solution
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Aiven makes it trival to move databases between cloud providers. The stickiest thing about any hyperscaler (AWS/Google/Azure) is your data - it's a PAIN to move. Aiven makes changing your cloud provider a literal point and shoot operation, something that could take weeks of planning trying to from RDS to Cloud SQL or the like.
cons: A little more customization in the pricing would be nice (and the ability to run within a VPC instead of having to peer VPCs), but honestly there's very little I don't like about Aiven.
